1	`timescale 1ns / 1ps
2	
3	/**
4	 * radar_system_top.v
5	 * 
6	 * Complete Radar System Top Module
7	 * Integrates:
8	 * - Radar Transmitter (PLFM chirp generation)
9	 * - Radar Receiver (ADC interface, DDC, matched filtering, Doppler processing)
10	 * - USB Data Interface (FT601 USB 3.0 or FT2232H USB 2.0, selected by USB_MODE)
11	 * 
12	 * Clock domains:
13	 * - clk_100m: System clock (100MHz)
14	 * - clk_120m_dac: DAC clock (120MHz)
15	 * - ft601_clk: USB interface clock (100MHz FT601 or 60MHz FT2232H)
16	 *
17	 * USB_MODE parameter:
18	 *   0 = FT601 (32-bit, USB 3.0) — 200T premium board
19	 *   1 = FT2232H (8-bit, USB 2.0) — 50T production board
20	 */
21	
22	module radar_system_top (
23	    // System Clocks
24	    input wire clk_100m,                // 100MHz system clock
25	    input wire clk_120m_dac,             // 120MHz DAC clock
26	    input wire ft601_clk_in,             // FT601 clock (100MHz)
27	    input wire reset_n,                   // Active-low reset
28	    
29	    // ========== TRANSMITTER INTERFACES ==========
30	    
31	    // DAC Interface
32	    output wire [7:0] dac_data,
33	    output wire dac_clk,
34	    output wire dac_sleep,
35	    
36	    // RF Switch Control
37	    output wire fpga_rf_switch,
38	    
39	    // Mixer Enables
40	    output wire rx_mixer_en,
41	    output wire tx_mixer_en,
42	    
43	    // ADAR1000 Beamformer Control (via level shifters)
44	    output wire adar_tx_load_1, adar_rx_load_1,
45	    output wire adar_tx_load_2, adar_rx_load_2,
46	    output wire adar_tx_load_3, adar_rx_load_3,
47	    output wire adar_tx_load_4, adar_rx_load_4,
48	    output wire adar_tr_1, adar_tr_2, adar_tr_3, adar_tr_4,
49	    
50	    // Level Shifter SPI Interface (STM32F7 to ADAR1000)
51	    input wire stm32_sclk_3v3,
52	    input wire stm32_mosi_3v3,
53	    output wire stm32_miso_3v3,
54	    input wire stm32_cs_adar1_3v3, stm32_cs_adar2_3v3, 
55	    input wire stm32_cs_adar3_3v3, stm32_cs_adar4_3v3,
56	    
57	    output wire stm32_sclk_1v8,
58	    output wire stm32_mosi_1v8,
59	    input wire stm32_miso_1v8,
60	    output wire stm32_cs_adar1_1v8, stm32_cs_adar2_1v8,
61	    output wire stm32_cs_adar3_1v8, stm32_cs_adar4_1v8,
62	    
63	    // ========== RECEIVER INTERFACES ==========
64	    
65	    // ADC Physical Interface (LVDS)
66	    input wire [7:0] adc_d_p,            // ADC Data P (LVDS)
67	    input wire [7:0] adc_d_n,            // ADC Data N (LVDS)
68	    input wire adc_dco_p,                 // Data Clock Output P (400MHz LVDS)
69	    input wire adc_dco_n,                 // Data Clock Output N (400MHz LVDS)
70	    output wire adc_pwdn,                  // ADC Power Down
71	    
72	    // ========== STM32 CONTROL INTERFACES ==========
73	    
74	    // Chirp/Beam Control (toggle signals from STM32)
75	    input wire stm32_new_chirp,
76	    input wire stm32_new_elevation,
77	    input wire stm32_new_azimuth,
78	    input wire stm32_mixers_enable,
79	    
80	    // ========== FT601 USB 3.0 INTERFACE ==========
81	    
82	    // Data bus
83	    inout wire [31:0] ft601_data,         // 32-bit bidirectional data bus
84	    output wire [3:0] ft601_be,            // Byte enable (4 lanes for 32-bit mode)
85	    
86	    // Control signals
87	    output wire ft601_txe_n,                // Transmit enable (active low)
88	    output wire ft601_rxf_n,                // Receive enable (active low)
89	    input wire ft601_txe,                    // Transmit FIFO empty
90	    input wire ft601_rxf,                    // Receive FIFO full
91	    output wire ft601_wr_n,                  // Write strobe (active low)
92	    output wire ft601_rd_n,                  // Read strobe (active low)
93	    output wire ft601_oe_n,                  // Output enable (active low)
94	    output wire ft601_siwu_n,                 // Send immediate / Wakeup
95	    
96	    // FIFO flags
97	    input wire [1:0] ft601_srb,              // Selected read buffer
98	    input wire [1:0] ft601_swb,               // Selected write buffer
99	    
100	    // Clock output (optional, FT601 only — not used for FT2232H)
101	    output wire ft601_clk_out,
102	    
103	    // ========== FT2232H USB 2.0 INTERFACE (USB_MODE=1) ==========
104	    // 8-bit bidirectional data bus (245 Synchronous FIFO mode, Channel A)
105	    inout wire [7:0] ft_data,            // 8-bit bidirectional data bus
106	    input wire ft_rxf_n,                  // RX FIFO not empty (active low)
107	    input wire ft_txe_n,                  // TX FIFO not full (active low)
108	    output wire ft_rd_n,                  // Read strobe (active low)
109	    output wire ft_wr_n,                  // Write strobe (active low)
110	    output wire ft_oe_n,                  // Output enable / bus direction
111	    output wire ft_siwu,                  // Send Immediate / WakeUp
112	
113	    // ========== STATUS OUTPUTS ==========
114	    
115	    // Beam position tracking
116	    output wire [5:0] current_elevation,
117	    output wire [5:0] current_azimuth,
118	    output wire [5:0] current_chirp,
119	    output wire new_chirp_frame,
120	    
121	    // Doppler processing outputs (for debugging)
122	    output wire [31:0] dbg_doppler_data,
123	    output wire dbg_doppler_valid,
124	    output wire [4:0] dbg_doppler_bin,
125	    output wire [5:0] dbg_range_bin,
126	    
127	    // System status
128	    output wire [3:0] system_status,
129	
130	    // FPGA→STM32 GPIO outputs (DIG_5..DIG_7 on 50T board)
131	    // Used by STM32 outer AGC loop to read saturation state without USB polling.
132	    output wire gpio_dig5,          // DIG_5 (H11→PD13): AGC saturation flag (1=clipping detected)
133	    output wire gpio_dig6,          // DIG_6 (G12→PD14): AGC enable flag (mirrors host_agc_enable)
134	    output wire gpio_dig7           // DIG_7 (H12→PD15): reserved (tied low)
135	);
136	
137	// ============================================================================
138	// PARAMETERS
139	// ============================================================================
140	
141	// System configuration
142	parameter USE_LONG_CHIRP = 1'b1;          // Default to long chirp
143	parameter DOPPLER_ENABLE = 1'b1;           // Enable Doppler processing
144	parameter USB_ENABLE = 1'b1;               // Enable USB data transfer
145	parameter USB_MODE = 1;                    // 0=FT601 (32-bit, 200T), 1=FT2232H (8-bit, 50T production default)
146	
147	// ============================================================================
148	// INTERNAL SIGNALS
149	// ============================================================================
150	
151	// Clock and reset
152	wire clk_100m_buf;
153	wire clk_120m_dac_buf;
154	wire ft601_clk_buf;
155	wire sys_reset_n;
156	wire sys_reset_120m_n;  // Reset synchronized to clk_120m_dac domain
157	wire sys_reset_ft601_n; // Reset synchronized to ft601_clk domain
158	
159	// CDC: synchronized versions of async inputs for status_reg
160	wire stm32_mixers_enable_100m;  // stm32_mixers_enable sync'd to clk_100m
161	wire ft601_txe_100m;            // ft601_txe sync'd to clk_100m
162	
163	// Transmitter internal signals
164	wire [7:0] tx_chirp_data;
165	wire tx_chirp_valid;
166	wire tx_chirp_done;
167	wire tx_new_chirp_frame;        // In clk_120m_dac domain
168	wire tx_new_chirp_frame_sync;   // Synchronized to clk_100m domain
169	wire [5:0] tx_current_elevation;
170	wire [5:0] tx_current_azimuth;
171	wire [5:0] tx_current_chirp;       // In clk_120m_dac domain
172	wire [5:0] tx_current_chirp_sync;  // Synchronized to clk_100m domain
173	wire tx_current_chirp_sync_valid;
174	
175	// Receiver internal signals
176	wire [31:0] rx_doppler_output;
177	wire rx_doppler_valid;
178	wire [4:0] rx_doppler_bin;
179	wire [5:0] rx_range_bin;
180	wire [31:0] rx_range_profile;
181	wire rx_range_valid;
182	wire [15:0] rx_doppler_real;
183	wire [15:0] rx_doppler_imag;
184	wire rx_doppler_data_valid;
185	reg rx_detect_flag;       // Threshold detection result (was rx_cfar_detection)
186	reg rx_detect_valid;      // Detection valid pulse (was rx_cfar_valid)
187	
188	// Frame-complete signal from Doppler processor (for CFAR)
189	wire rx_frame_complete;
190	
191	// ADC debug tap from receiver (clk_100m domain, post-DDC)
192	wire [15:0] rx_dbg_adc_i;
193	wire [15:0] rx_dbg_adc_q;
194	wire        rx_dbg_adc_valid;
195	
196	// AGC status from receiver (for status readback and GPIO)
197	wire [7:0]  rx_agc_saturation_count;
198	wire [7:0]  rx_agc_peak_magnitude;
199	wire [3:0]  rx_agc_current_gain;
200	
201	// Data packing for USB
202	wire [31:0] usb_range_profile;
203	wire usb_range_valid;
204	wire [15:0] usb_doppler_real;
205	wire [15:0] usb_doppler_imag;
206	wire usb_doppler_valid;
207	wire usb_detect_flag;     // (was usb_cfar_detection)
208	wire usb_detect_valid;    // (was usb_cfar_valid)
209	
210	// System status
211	reg [3:0] status_reg;
212	
213	// USB host command outputs (Gap 4: USB Read Path)
214	// These are in the ft601_clk domain; CDC'd to clk_100m below
215	wire [31:0] usb_cmd_data;
216	wire        usb_cmd_valid;     // 1-cycle pulse in ft601_clk domain
217	wire [7:0]  usb_cmd_opcode;
218	wire [7:0]  usb_cmd_addr;
219	wire [15:0] usb_cmd_value;
220	
221	// USB command decode registers (clk_100m domain, driven by CDC block below)
222	// Declared here (before rx_inst) so Icarus Verilog can resolve forward refs.
223	reg [1:0]  host_radar_mode;
224	reg        host_trigger_pulse;
225	reg [15:0] host_detect_threshold;  // (was host_cfar_threshold)
226	reg [2:0]  host_stream_control;
227	
228	// Fix 3: Digital gain control register
229	// [3]=direction: 0=amplify, 1=attenuate. [2:0]=shift amount 0..7.
230	// Default 0x00 = pass-through (no gain change).
231	reg [3:0]  host_gain_shift;
232	
233	// Gap 2: Host-configurable chirp timing registers
234	// These override the compile-time defaults in radar_mode_controller when
235	// written via USB command. Defaults match the parameter values in
236	// radar_mode_controller.v so behavior is unchanged until the host writes them.
237	reg [15:0] host_long_chirp_cycles;    // Opcode 0x10 (default 3000)
238	reg [15:0] host_long_listen_cycles;   // Opcode 0x11 (default 13700)
239	reg [15:0] host_guard_cycles;         // Opcode 0x12 (default 17540)
240	reg [15:0] host_short_chirp_cycles;   // Opcode 0x13 (default 50)
241	reg [15:0] host_short_listen_cycles;  // Opcode 0x14 (default 17450)
242	reg [5:0]  host_chirps_per_elev;      // Opcode 0x15 (default 32)
243	reg        host_status_request;       // Opcode 0xFF (self-clearing pulse)
244	
245	// Fix 4: Doppler/chirps mismatch protection
246	// DOPPLER_FRAME_CHIRPS is the fixed chirp count expected by the staggered-PRI
247	// Doppler path (16 long + 16 short). If host sets chirps_per_elev to a
248	// different value, Doppler accumulation is corrupted. Clamp at command decode
249	// and flag the mismatch so the host knows.
250	localparam DOPPLER_FRAME_CHIRPS = 32; // Total chirps per Doppler frame
251	reg        chirps_mismatch_error;     // Set if host tried to set chirps != FFT size
252	
253	// Fix 7: Range-mode register (opcode 0x20)
254	// Future-proofing for 3km/10km antenna switching.
255	//   2'b00 = Auto (default — system selects based on scene)
256	//   2'b01 = Short-range (3km)
257	//   2'b10 = Long-range (10km)
258	//   2'b11 = Reserved
259	// Currently a configuration store only — antenna/timing switching TBD.
260	reg [1:0]  host_range_mode;
261	
262	// CFAR configuration registers (host-configurable via USB)
263	reg [3:0]  host_cfar_guard;       // Opcode 0x21: guard cells per side (0..8)
264	reg [4:0]  host_cfar_train;       // Opcode 0x22: training cells per side (1..16)
265	reg [7:0]  host_cfar_alpha;       // Opcode 0x23: threshold multiplier (Q4.4)
266	reg [1:0]  host_cfar_mode;        // Opcode 0x24: 00=CA, 01=GO, 10=SO
267	reg        host_cfar_enable;      // Opcode 0x25: 1=CFAR, 0=simple threshold
268	
269	// Ground clutter removal registers (host-configurable via USB)
270	reg        host_mti_enable;       // Opcode 0x26: 1=MTI active, 0=pass-through
271	reg [2:0]  host_dc_notch_width;   // Opcode 0x27: DC notch ±width bins (0=off, 1..7)
272	
273	// AGC configuration registers (host-configurable via USB, opcodes 0x28-0x2C)
274	reg        host_agc_enable;       // Opcode 0x28: 0=manual gain, 1=auto AGC
275	reg [7:0]  host_agc_target;       // Opcode 0x29: target peak magnitude (default 200)
276	reg [3:0]  host_agc_attack;       // Opcode 0x2A: gain-down step on clipping (default 1)
277	reg [3:0]  host_agc_decay;        // Opcode 0x2B: gain-up step when weak (default 1)
278	reg [3:0]  host_agc_holdoff;      // Opcode 0x2C: frames to wait before gain-up (default 4)
279	
280	// Board bring-up self-test registers (opcode 0x30 trigger, 0x31 readback)
281	reg        host_self_test_trigger;  // Opcode 0x30: self-clearing pulse
282	wire       self_test_busy;
283	wire       self_test_result_valid;
284	wire [4:0] self_test_result_flags;  // Per-test PASS(1)/FAIL(0)
285	wire [7:0] self_test_result_detail; // Diagnostic detail byte
286	// Self-test latched results (hold until next trigger)
287	reg  [4:0] self_test_flags_latched;
288	reg  [7:0] self_test_detail_latched;
289	// Self-test ADC capture wires
290	wire       self_test_capture_active;
291	wire [15:0] self_test_capture_data;
292	wire       self_test_capture_valid;
293	
294	// ============================================================================
295	// CLOCK BUFFERING
296	// ============================================================================
297	
298	`ifdef SIMULATION
299	// In simulation (iverilog), BUFG is not available — pass-through assigns
300	assign clk_100m_buf     = clk_100m;
301	assign clk_120m_dac_buf = clk_120m_dac;
302	assign ft601_clk_buf    = ft601_clk_in;
303	`else
304	BUFG bufg_100m (
305	    .I(clk_100m),
306	    .O(clk_100m_buf)
307	);
308	
309	BUFG bufg_120m (
310	    .I(clk_120m_dac),
311	    .O(clk_120m_dac_buf)
312	);
313	
314	BUFG bufg_ft601 (
315	    .I(ft601_clk_in),
316	    .O(ft601_clk_buf)
317	);
318	`endif
319	
320	// Reset synchronization (clk_100m domain)
321	(* ASYNC_REG = "TRUE" *) reg [1:0] reset_sync;
322	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf or negedge reset_n) begin
323	    if (!reset_n) begin
324	        reset_sync <= 2'b00;
325	    end else begin
326	        reset_sync <= {reset_sync[0], 1'b1};
327	    end
328	end
329	assign sys_reset_n = reset_sync[1];
330	
331	// Reset synchronization (clk_120m_dac domain)
332	// Ensures reset deassertion is synchronous to the DAC clock,
333	// preventing recovery/removal timing violations on 120 MHz FFs.
334	(* ASYNC_REG = "TRUE" *) reg [1:0] reset_sync_120m;
335	always @(posedge clk_120m_dac_buf or negedge reset_n) begin
336	    if (!reset_n) begin
337	        reset_sync_120m <= 2'b00;
338	    end else begin
339	        reset_sync_120m <= {reset_sync_120m[0], 1'b1};
340	    end
341	end
342	assign sys_reset_120m_n = reset_sync_120m[1];
343	
344	// Reset synchronization (ft601_clk domain)
345	// FT601 has its own asynchronous clock from the USB controller.
346	// All FT601-domain registers need a properly synchronized reset.
347	(* ASYNC_REG = "TRUE" *) reg [2:0] reset_sync_ft601;  // 3-stage for better MTBF
348	always @(posedge ft601_clk_buf or negedge reset_n) begin
349	    if (!reset_n) begin
350	        reset_sync_ft601 <= 3'b000;
351	    end else begin
352	        reset_sync_ft601 <= {reset_sync_ft601[1:0], 1'b1};
353	    end
354	end
355	assign sys_reset_ft601_n = reset_sync_ft601[2];
356	
357	// CDC synchronizers for status_reg inputs (async -> clk_100m)
358	// stm32_mixers_enable is an async GPIO; ft601_txe is on ft601_clk domain
359	cdc_single_bit #(.STAGES(2)) cdc_mixers_en_status (
360	    .src_clk(clk_100m_buf),           // Pseudo-source for async GPIO
361	    .dst_clk(clk_100m_buf),
362	    .reset_n(sys_reset_n),
363	    .src_signal(stm32_mixers_enable),
364	    .dst_signal(stm32_mixers_enable_100m)
365	);
366	
367	cdc_single_bit #(.STAGES(2)) cdc_ft601_txe_status (
368	    .src_clk(ft601_clk_buf),
369	    .dst_clk(clk_100m_buf),
370	    .reset_n(sys_reset_n),
371	    .src_signal(ft601_txe),
372	    .dst_signal(ft601_txe_100m)
373	);
374	
375	// ============================================================================
376	// CLOCK DOMAIN CROSSING: TRANSMITTER (120 MHz) -> SYSTEM (100 MHz)
377	// ============================================================================
378	
379	// CDC for chirp_counter: 6-bit multi-bit Gray-code synchronizer
380	// Source domain is clk_120m_dac, so reset must be synchronized to that domain.
381	// The cdc_adc_to_processing module uses synchronous reset internally, so
382	// using sys_reset_120m_n (120m-synchronized) is correct for the source side.
383	// The destination side will sample it synchronously on dst_clk, which at worst
384	// delays reset deassertion by 1-2 cycles — acceptable for CDC reset.
385	cdc_adc_to_processing #(
386	    .WIDTH(6),
387	    .STAGES(3)
388	) cdc_chirp_counter (
389	    .src_clk(clk_120m_dac_buf),
390	    .dst_clk(clk_100m_buf),
391	    .src_reset_n(sys_reset_120m_n),
392	    .dst_reset_n(sys_reset_n),
393	    .src_data(tx_current_chirp),
394	    .src_valid(1'b1),           // Always valid — counter updates continuously
395	    .dst_data(tx_current_chirp_sync),
396	    .dst_valid(tx_current_chirp_sync_valid)
397	);
398	
399	// CDC for new_chirp_frame: toggle CDC (pulse on clk_120m -> pulse on clk_100m)
400	// new_chirp_frame is a 1-cycle pulse on clk_120m_dac. A level synchronizer
401	// at 100 MHz can miss it. Toggle CDC converts pulse -> level toggle,
402	// synchronizes the toggle, then detects edges to recover the pulse.
403	reg chirp_frame_toggle_120m;
404	always @(posedge clk_120m_dac_buf or negedge sys_reset_120m_n) begin
405	    if (!sys_reset_120m_n)
406	        chirp_frame_toggle_120m <= 1'b0;
407	    else if (tx_new_chirp_frame)
408	        chirp_frame_toggle_120m <= ~chirp_frame_toggle_120m;
409	end
410	
411	wire chirp_frame_toggle_100m;
412	cdc_single_bit #(
413	    .STAGES(3)
414	) cdc_new_chirp_frame (
415	    .src_clk(clk_120m_dac_buf),
416	    .dst_clk(clk_100m_buf),
417	    .reset_n(sys_reset_n),
418	    .src_signal(chirp_frame_toggle_120m),
419	    .dst_signal(chirp_frame_toggle_100m)
420	);
421	
422	reg chirp_frame_toggle_100m_prev;
423	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf or negedge sys_reset_n) begin
424	    if (!sys_reset_n)
425	        chirp_frame_toggle_100m_prev <= 1'b0;
426	    else
427	        chirp_frame_toggle_100m_prev <= chirp_frame_toggle_100m;
428	end
429	assign tx_new_chirp_frame_sync = chirp_frame_toggle_100m ^ chirp_frame_toggle_100m_prev;
430	
431	// ============================================================================
432	// RADAR TRANSMITTER INSTANTIATION
433	// ============================================================================
434	
435	radar_transmitter tx_inst (
436	    // System Clocks
437	    .clk_100m(clk_100m_buf),
438	    .clk_120m_dac(clk_120m_dac_buf),
439	    .reset_n(sys_reset_120m_n),    // 120 MHz-synchronized reset for DAC-domain logic
440	    .reset_100m_n(sys_reset_n),    // 100 MHz-synchronized reset for edge detectors/CDC
441	    
442	    // DAC Interface
443	    .dac_data(dac_data),
444	    .dac_clk(dac_clk),
445	    .dac_sleep(dac_sleep),
446	    
447	    // Mixer Enables
448	    .rx_mixer_en(rx_mixer_en),
449	    .tx_mixer_en(tx_mixer_en),
450	    
451	    // STM32 Control Interface
452	    .stm32_new_chirp(stm32_new_chirp),
453	    .stm32_new_elevation(stm32_new_elevation),
454	    .stm32_new_azimuth(stm32_new_azimuth),
455	    .stm32_mixers_enable(stm32_mixers_enable),
456	    
457	    // RF Switch Control
458	    .fpga_rf_switch(fpga_rf_switch),
459	    
460	    // ADAR1000 Control Interface
461	    .adar_tx_load_1(adar_tx_load_1),
462	    .adar_rx_load_1(adar_rx_load_1),
463	    .adar_tx_load_2(adar_tx_load_2),
464	    .adar_rx_load_2(adar_rx_load_2),
465	    .adar_tx_load_3(adar_tx_load_3),
466	    .adar_rx_load_3(adar_rx_load_3),
467	    .adar_tx_load_4(adar_tx_load_4),

487	    .stm32_cs_adar2_1v8(stm32_cs_adar2_1v8),
488	    .stm32_cs_adar3_1v8(stm32_cs_adar3_1v8),
489	    .stm32_cs_adar4_1v8(stm32_cs_adar4_1v8),

512	    .adc_d_p(adc_d_p),
513	    .adc_d_n(adc_d_n),
514	    .adc_dco_p(adc_dco_p),
515	    .adc_dco_n(adc_dco_n),
516	    .adc_pwdn(adc_pwdn),
517

519	    .doppler_output(rx_doppler_output),

521	    .doppler_bin(rx_doppler_bin),
522	    .range_bin(rx_range_bin),
523

525	    .range_profile_i_out(rx_range_profile[15:0]),
526	    .range_profile_q_out(rx_range_profile[31:16]),
527	    .range_profile_valid_out(rx_range_valid),
528

530	    .host_mode(host_radar_mode),
531	    .host_trigger(host_trigger_pulse),
532	    // Gap 2: Host-configurable chirp timing
533	    .host_long_chirp_cycles(host_long_chirp_cycles),
534	    .host_long_listen_cycles(host_long_listen_cycles),
535	    .host_guard_cycles(host_guard_cycles),
536	    .host_short_chirp_cycles(host_short_chirp_cycles),
537	    .host_short_listen_cycles(host_short_listen_cycles),
538	    .host_chirps_per_elev(host_chirps_per_elev),
539	    // Fix 3: digital gain control
540	    .host_gain_shift(host_gain_shift),

542	    .host_agc_enable(host_agc_enable),
543	    .host_agc_target(host_agc_target),
544	    .host_agc_attack(host_agc_attack),
545	    .host_agc_decay(host_agc_decay),
546	    .host_agc_holdoff(host_agc_holdoff),

566	);
567	
568	// ============================================================================
569	// DOPPLER DATA DECODING
570	// ============================================================================
571	
572	// Decode 32-bit doppler output into real and imaginary parts
573	// Format: {doppler_q[15:0], doppler_i[15:0]}
574	assign rx_doppler_real = rx_doppler_output[15:0];
575	assign rx_doppler_imag = rx_doppler_output[31:16];
576	assign rx_doppler_data_valid = rx_doppler_valid;
577	
578	// ============================================================================
579	// DC NOTCH FILTER (post-Doppler-FFT, pre-CFAR)
580	// ============================================================================
581	// Zeros out Doppler bins within ±host_dc_notch_width of DC for BOTH
582	// sub-frames in the dual 16-pt FFT architecture.
583	// doppler_bin[4:0] = {sub_frame, bin[3:0]}:
584	//   Sub-frame 0: bins 0-15,  DC = bin 0,  wrap = bin 15
585	//   Sub-frame 1: bins 16-31, DC = bin 16, wrap = bin 31
586	// notch_width=1 → zero bins {0,16}. notch_width=2 → zero bins
587	// {0,1,15,16,17,31}. etc.
588	// When host_dc_notch_width=0: pass-through (no zeroing).
589	
590	wire dc_notch_active;
591	wire [4:0] dop_bin_unsigned = rx_doppler_bin;
592	wire [3:0] bin_within_sf = dop_bin_unsigned[3:0];
593	assign dc_notch_active = (host_dc_notch_width != 3'd0) &&
594	                          (bin_within_sf < {1'b0, host_dc_notch_width} ||
595	                           bin_within_sf > (4'd15 - {1'b0, host_dc_notch_width} + 4'd1));
596	
597	// Notched Doppler data: zero I/Q when in notch zone, pass through otherwise
598	wire [31:0] notched_doppler_data  = dc_notch_active ? 32'd0 : rx_doppler_output;
599	wire        notched_doppler_valid = rx_doppler_valid;
600	wire [4:0]  notched_doppler_bin   = rx_doppler_bin;
601	wire [5:0]  notched_range_bin     = rx_range_bin;
602	
603	// ============================================================================
604	// CFAR DETECTOR (replaces simple threshold detector)
605	// ============================================================================
606	// Cell-Averaging CFAR with CA/GO/SO modes. When cfg_cfar_enable=0,
607	// falls back to simple magnitude threshold (backward-compatible).
608	// See cfar_ca.v for architecture details.
609	
610	wire cfar_detect_flag;
611	wire cfar_detect_valid;
612	wire [5:0]  cfar_detect_range;
613	wire [4:0]  cfar_detect_doppler;
614	wire [16:0] cfar_detect_magnitude;
615	wire [16:0] cfar_detect_threshold;
616	wire [15:0] cfar_detect_count;
617	wire        cfar_busy_w;
618	wire [7:0]  cfar_status_w;
619	
620	cfar_ca cfar_inst (
621	    .clk(clk_100m_buf),
622	    .reset_n(sys_reset_n),
623	
624	    // Doppler processor outputs (DC-notch filtered)
625	    .doppler_data(notched_doppler_data),
626	    .doppler_valid(notched_doppler_valid),
627	    .doppler_bin_in(notched_doppler_bin),
628	    .range_bin_in(notched_range_bin),
629	    .frame_complete(rx_frame_complete),
630	
631	    // Configuration
632	    .cfg_guard_cells(host_cfar_guard),
633	    .cfg_train_cells(host_cfar_train),
634	    .cfg_alpha(host_cfar_alpha),
635	    .cfg_cfar_mode(host_cfar_mode),
636	    .cfg_cfar_enable(host_cfar_enable),
637	    .cfg_simple_threshold(host_detect_threshold),
638	
639	    // Detection outputs
640	    .detect_flag(cfar_detect_flag),
641	    .detect_valid(cfar_detect_valid),
642	    .detect_range(cfar_detect_range),
643	    .detect_doppler(cfar_detect_doppler),
644	    .detect_magnitude(cfar_detect_magnitude),
645	    .detect_threshold(cfar_detect_threshold),
646	
647	    // Status
648	    .detect_count(cfar_detect_count),
649	    .cfar_busy(cfar_busy_w),
650	    .cfar_status(cfar_status_w)
651	);
652	
653	// Connect CFAR outputs to existing detection signals
654	// (rx_detect_flag/valid are regs — drive them from CFAR combinationally)
655	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf or negedge sys_reset_n) begin
656	    if (!sys_reset_n) begin
657	        rx_detect_flag  <= 1'b0;
658	        rx_detect_valid <= 1'b0;
659	    end else begin
660	        rx_detect_flag  <= cfar_detect_flag;
661	        rx_detect_valid <= cfar_detect_valid;
662	    end
663	end
664	
665	// ============================================================================
666	// BOARD BRING-UP SELF-TEST (opcode 0x30 trigger, 0x31 readback)
667	// ============================================================================
668	// Exercises key subsystems independently on first power-on.
669	// ADC data input is tied to real ADC data.
670	
671	fpga_self_test self_test_inst (
672	    .clk(clk_100m_buf),
673	    .reset_n(sys_reset_n),
674	    .trigger(host_self_test_trigger),
675	    .busy(self_test_busy),
676	    .result_valid(self_test_result_valid),
677	    .result_flags(self_test_result_flags),
678	    .result_detail(self_test_result_detail),
679	    .adc_data_in(rx_dbg_adc_i),   // Post-DDC I channel (clk_100m, 16-bit signed)
680	    .adc_valid_in(rx_dbg_adc_valid), // DDC output valid (clk_100m)
681	    .capture_active(self_test_capture_active),
682	    .capture_data(self_test_capture_data),
683	    .capture_valid(self_test_capture_valid)
684	);
685	
686	// Latch self-test results when valid (hold until next trigger)
687	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf or negedge sys_reset_n) begin
688	    if (!sys_reset_n) begin
689	        self_test_flags_latched  <= 5'b00000;
690	        self_test_detail_latched <= 8'd0;
691	    end else begin
692	        if (self_test_result_valid) begin
693	            self_test_flags_latched  <= self_test_result_flags;
694	            self_test_detail_latched <= self_test_result_detail;
695	        end
696	    end
697	end

833	        .status_long_chirp(host_long_chirp_cycles),

855	    assign ft601_txe_n  = 1'b1;

857	    assign ft601_wr_n   = 1'b1;
858	    assign ft601_rd_n   = 1'b1;

863	endgenerate
864	
865	// ============================================================================
866	// USB COMMAND CDC: ft601_clk → clk_100m (Gap 4: USB Read Path)
867	// ============================================================================
868	// cmd_valid is a 1-cycle pulse in ft601_clk. Use toggle CDC (same pattern
869	// as chirp_frame_toggle_120m above) to safely transfer it to clk_100m.
870	// cmd_data/opcode/addr/value are held stable after cmd_valid pulses, so
871	// we simply sample them in clk_100m when the CDC'd pulse arrives.
872	
873	// Step 1: Toggle on cmd_valid pulse (ft601_clk domain)
874	reg cmd_valid_toggle_ft601;
875	always @(posedge ft601_clk_buf or negedge sys_reset_ft601_n) begin
876	    if (!sys_reset_ft601_n)
877	        cmd_valid_toggle_ft601 <= 1'b0;
878	    else if (usb_cmd_valid)
879	        cmd_valid_toggle_ft601 <= ~cmd_valid_toggle_ft601;
880	end
881	
882	// Step 2: Synchronize toggle to clk_100m domain (3-stage)
883	wire cmd_valid_toggle_100m;
884	cdc_single_bit #(
885	    .STAGES(3)
886	) cdc_cmd_valid (
887	    .src_clk(ft601_clk_buf),
888	    .dst_clk(clk_100m_buf),
889	    .reset_n(sys_reset_n),
890	    .src_signal(cmd_valid_toggle_ft601),
891	    .dst_signal(cmd_valid_toggle_100m)
892	);
893	
894	// Step 3: Edge-detect toggle to recover pulse in clk_100m domain
895	reg cmd_valid_toggle_100m_prev;
896	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf or negedge sys_reset_n) begin
897	    if (!sys_reset_n)
898	        cmd_valid_toggle_100m_prev <= 1'b0;
899	    else
900	        cmd_valid_toggle_100m_prev <= cmd_valid_toggle_100m;
901	end
902	wire cmd_valid_100m = cmd_valid_toggle_100m ^ cmd_valid_toggle_100m_prev;
903	
904	// Step 4: Command decode registers in clk_100m domain
905	// Sample cmd_data fields when CDC'd valid pulse arrives. Data is stable
906	// because the read FSM holds cmd_opcode/addr/value until the next command.
907	// NOTE: reg declarations for host_radar_mode, host_trigger_pulse,
908	// host_detect_threshold, host_stream_control are in INTERNAL SIGNALS section
909	// above (before rx_inst) to avoid Icarus Verilog forward-reference errors.
910	
911	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf or negedge sys_reset_n) begin
912	    if (!sys_reset_n) begin
913	        host_radar_mode    <= 2'b01;   // Default: auto-scan
914	        host_trigger_pulse <= 1'b0;
915	        host_detect_threshold <= 16'd10000; // Default threshold
916	        host_stream_control <= 3'b111;    // Default: all streams enabled
917	        host_gain_shift     <= 4'd0;      // Default: pass-through (no gain change)
918	        // Gap 2: chirp timing defaults (match radar_mode_controller parameters)
919	        host_long_chirp_cycles  <= 16'd3000;
920	        host_long_listen_cycles <= 16'd13700;
921	        host_guard_cycles       <= 16'd17540;
922	        host_short_chirp_cycles <= 16'd50;
923	        host_short_listen_cycles <= 16'd17450;
924	        host_chirps_per_elev    <= 6'd32;
925	        host_status_request     <= 1'b0;
926	        chirps_mismatch_error   <= 1'b0;
927	        host_range_mode         <= 2'b00;     // Default: auto
928	        // CFAR defaults (disabled by default — backward-compatible)
929	        host_cfar_guard         <= 4'd2;      // 2 guard cells each side
930	        host_cfar_train         <= 5'd8;      // 8 training cells each side
931	        host_cfar_alpha         <= 8'h30;     // alpha=3.0 (Q4.4)
932	        host_cfar_mode          <= 2'b00;     // CA-CFAR
933	        host_cfar_enable        <= 1'b0;      // Disabled (simple threshold)
934	        // Ground clutter removal defaults (disabled — backward-compatible)
935	        host_mti_enable         <= 1'b0;      // MTI off
936	        host_dc_notch_width     <= 3'd0;      // DC notch off
937	        // AGC defaults (disabled — backward-compatible with manual gain)
938	        host_agc_enable         <= 1'b0;      // AGC off (manual gain)
939	        host_agc_target         <= 8'd200;    // Target peak magnitude
940	        host_agc_attack         <= 4'd1;      // 1-step gain-down on clipping
941	        host_agc_decay          <= 4'd1;      // 1-step gain-up when weak
942	        host_agc_holdoff        <= 4'd4;      // 4 frames before gain-up
943	        // Self-test defaults
944	        host_self_test_trigger  <= 1'b0;      // Self-test idle
945	    end else begin
946	        host_trigger_pulse <= 1'b0;    // Self-clearing pulse
947	        host_status_request <= 1'b0;   // Self-clearing pulse
948	        host_self_test_trigger <= 1'b0;  // Self-clearing pulse
949	        if (cmd_valid_100m) begin
950	            case (usb_cmd_opcode)
951	                8'h01: host_radar_mode     <= usb_cmd_value[1:0];
952	                8'h02: host_trigger_pulse  <= 1'b1;
953	                8'h03: host_detect_threshold <= usb_cmd_value;
954	                8'h04: host_stream_control <= usb_cmd_value[2:0];
955	                // Gap 2: chirp timing configuration
956	                8'h10: host_long_chirp_cycles  <= usb_cmd_value;
957	                8'h11: host_long_listen_cycles <= usb_cmd_value;
958	                8'h12: host_guard_cycles       <= usb_cmd_value;
959	                8'h13: host_short_chirp_cycles <= usb_cmd_value;
960	                8'h14: host_short_listen_cycles <= usb_cmd_value;
961	                8'h15: begin
962	                    // Fix 4: Clamp chirps_per_elev to the fixed Doppler frame size.
963	                    // If host requests a different value, clamp and set error flag.
964	                    if (usb_cmd_value[5:0] > DOPPLER_FRAME_CHIRPS[5:0]) begin
965	                        host_chirps_per_elev  <= DOPPLER_FRAME_CHIRPS[5:0];
966	                        chirps_mismatch_error <= 1'b1;
967	                    end else if (usb_cmd_value[5:0] == 6'd0) begin
968	                        host_chirps_per_elev  <= DOPPLER_FRAME_CHIRPS[5:0];
969	                        chirps_mismatch_error <= 1'b1;
970	                    end else begin
971	                        host_chirps_per_elev  <= usb_cmd_value[5:0];
972	                        // Clear error only if value matches FFT size exactly
973	                        chirps_mismatch_error <= (usb_cmd_value[5:0] != DOPPLER_FRAME_CHIRPS[5:0]);
974	                    end
975	                end
976	                8'h16: host_gain_shift         <= usb_cmd_value[3:0];  // Fix 3: digital gain
977	                8'h20: host_range_mode         <= usb_cmd_value[1:0];  // Fix 7: range mode
978	                // CFAR configuration opcodes
979	                8'h21: host_cfar_guard         <= usb_cmd_value[3:0];
980	                8'h22: host_cfar_train         <= usb_cmd_value[4:0];
981	                8'h23: host_cfar_alpha         <= usb_cmd_value[7:0];
982	                8'h24: host_cfar_mode          <= usb_cmd_value[1:0];
983	                8'h25: host_cfar_enable        <= usb_cmd_value[0];
984	                // Ground clutter removal opcodes
985	                8'h26: host_mti_enable         <= usb_cmd_value[0];
986	                8'h27: host_dc_notch_width     <= usb_cmd_value[2:0];
987	                // AGC configuration opcodes
988	                8'h28: host_agc_enable         <= usb_cmd_value[0];
989	                8'h29: host_agc_target         <= usb_cmd_value[7:0];
990	                8'h2A: host_agc_attack         <= usb_cmd_value[3:0];
991	                8'h2B: host_agc_decay          <= usb_cmd_value[3:0];
992	                8'h2C: host_agc_holdoff        <= usb_cmd_value[3:0];
993	                // Board bring-up self-test opcodes
994	                8'h30: host_self_test_trigger  <= 1'b1;  // Trigger self-test
995	                8'h31: host_status_request     <= 1'b1;  // Self-test readback (status alias)
996	                // 0x31: readback handled via status mechanism (latched results)
997	                8'hFF: host_status_request     <= 1'b1;  // Gap 2: status readback
998	                default: ;
999	            endcase
1000	        end
1001	    end
1002	end
1003	
1004	// ============================================================================
1005	// OUTPUT ASSIGNMENTS
1006	// ============================================================================
1007	
1008	assign current_elevation = tx_current_elevation;
1009	assign current_azimuth = tx_current_azimuth;
1010	assign current_chirp = tx_current_chirp_sync;        // Use CDC-synchronized version
1011	assign new_chirp_frame = tx_new_chirp_frame_sync;    // Use CDC-synchronized version
1012	
1013	assign dbg_doppler_data = rx_doppler_output;
1014	assign dbg_doppler_valid = rx_doppler_valid;
1015	assign dbg_doppler_bin = rx_doppler_bin;
1016	assign dbg_range_bin = rx_range_bin;
1017	
1018	// ============================================================================
1019	// SYSTEM STATUS MONITORING
1020	// ============================================================================
1021	
1022	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf or negedge sys_reset_n) begin
1023	    if (!sys_reset_n) begin
1024	        status_reg <= 4'b0000;
1025	    end else begin
1026	        status_reg[0] <= stm32_mixers_enable_100m;  // Mixers enabled (CDC sync'd)
1027	        status_reg[1] <= ft601_txe_100m;             // USB TX ready (CDC sync'd)
1028	        status_reg[2] <= rx_doppler_valid;          // Data valid
1029	        status_reg[3] <= tx_new_chirp_frame_sync;   // New chirp frame (CDC-sync'd)
1030	    end
1031	end
1032	
1033	assign system_status = status_reg;
1034	
1035	// ============================================================================
1036	// FPGA→STM32 GPIO OUTPUTS (DIG_5, DIG_6, DIG_7)
1037	// ============================================================================
1038	// DIG_5: AGC saturation flag — high when per-frame saturation_count > 0.
1039	//        STM32 reads PD13 to detect clipping and adjust ADAR1000 VGA gain.
1040	// DIG_6: AGC enable flag — mirrors host_agc_enable so STM32 outer-loop AGC
1041	//        tracks the FPGA register as single source of truth.
1042	// DIG_7: Reserved (tied low for future use).
1043	assign gpio_dig5 = (rx_agc_saturation_count != 8'd0);
1044	assign gpio_dig6 = host_agc_enable;
1045	assign gpio_dig7 = 1'b0;
1046	
1047	// ============================================================================
1048	// DEBUG AND VERIFICATION
1049	// ============================================================================
1050	
1051	`ifdef SIMULATION
1052	// Simulation-only debug monitoring
1053	reg [31:0] debug_cycle_counter;
1054	reg [31:0] data_packet_counter;
1055	
1056	always @(posedge clk_100m_buf) begin
1057	    debug_cycle_counter <= debug_cycle_counter + 1;
1058	    
1059	    if (tx_new_chirp_frame_sync) begin
1060	        $display("[TOP] New chirp frame started at cycle %0d", debug_cycle_counter);
1061	    end
1062	    
1063	    if (rx_doppler_valid) begin
1064	        data_packet_counter <= data_packet_counter + 1;
1065	        if (data_packet_counter < 10) begin
1066	            $display("[TOP] Doppler data[%0d]: bin=%0d, range=%0d, I=%0d, Q=%0d",
1067	                     data_packet_counter, rx_doppler_bin, rx_range_bin,
1068	                     rx_doppler_real, rx_doppler_imag);
1069	        end
1070	    end
1071	    
1072	    if (data_packet_counter == 100) begin
1073	        $display("[TOP] First 100 doppler packets processed");
1074	    end
1075	end
1076	`endif
1077	
1078	endmodule
1079

1	# ============================================================================
2	# AERIS-10  FT601 via FMC LPC: TE0713 + TE0701 + UMFT601X-B
3	# ============================================================================
4	# Target: XC7A200T-2FBG484C (TE0713-03) on TE0701-06 carrier
5	# FT601 board: UMFT601X-B (32-bit FT601 eval, FMC LPC)
6	#
7	# Signal chain:
8	#   FPGA ball → TE0713 B2B → TE0701 carrier → FMC LPC J10 → UMFT601X-B FT601
9	#
10	# Bank split:
11	#   Bank 15 (VCCO = VIOTB): DATA[31:0], D_CLK (33 pins)
12	#   Bank 16 (VCCO = VIOTB): BE_N[3:0], OE_N, RD_N, WR_N, TXE_N, RXF_N,
13	#                            SIWU_N, RESET_N, WAKEUP_N, GPIO0, GPIO1 (14 pins)
14	#
15	# CRITICAL SETUP:
16	#   1. TE0701 VIOTB must be set to 3.3V (jumper J16/J17/J21 configuration)
17	#      OR FMC_VADJ (DIP S4) set to 3.3V with VIOTB routed to FMC_VADJ
18	#   2. UMFT601X-B VCCIO jumper set to 3.3V
19	#   3. This XDC replaces the FT601 section of xc7a200t_fbg484.xdc (production
20	#      PCB pinout) — do NOT use both simultaneously
21	#
22	# Source mapping:
23	#   UMFT601X-B: DS_UMFT60x.pdf Table 2.7 (CN4 FMC connector, FT601 column)
24	#   TE0701→TE0713: TE0701_FMC_PINOUT.xlsx (FMC J10 → B2B → FPGA pin)
25	#
26	# Verified: 2026-03-19
27	# ============================================================================
28	
29	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
30	# FT601 Clock Input — 100 MHz from FT601 chip
31	# FMC: LA18_P_CC → FPGA J20 (Bank 15, IO_L11P_T1_SRCC_15)
32	# SRCC is sufficient for 100 MHz FIFO clock via IBUF→BUFG
33	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	set_property PACKAGE_PIN J20 [get_ports {ft601_clk_in}]
35	set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_clk_in}]
36	create_clock -name ft601_clk_in -period 10.000 [get_ports {ft601_clk_in}]
37	set_input_jitter [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] 0.100
38	
39	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	# FT601 Data Bus [31:0] — bidirectional, 3.3V LVCMOS
41	# All data pins in Bank 15
42	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
43	# FMC LA32_N → L21
44	set_property PACKAGE_PIN L21 [get_ports {ft601_data[0]}]
45	# FMC LA33_N → N20
46	set_property PACKAGE_PIN N20 [get_ports {ft601_data[1]}]
47	# FMC LA32_P → M21
48	set_property PACKAGE_PIN M21 [get_ports {ft601_data[2]}]
49	# FMC LA33_P → M20
50	set_property PACKAGE_PIN M20 [get_ports {ft601_data[3]}]
51	# FMC LA30_N → M13
52	set_property PACKAGE_PIN M13 [get_ports {ft601_data[4]}]
53	# FMC LA31_N → N22
54	set_property PACKAGE_PIN N22 [get_ports {ft601_data[5]}]
55	# FMC LA30_P → L13
56	set_property PACKAGE_PIN L13 [get_ports {ft601_data[6]}]
57	# FMC LA31_P → M22
58	set_property PACKAGE_PIN M22 [get_ports {ft601_data[7]}]
59	# FMC LA28_N → H18
60	set_property PACKAGE_PIN H18 [get_ports {ft601_data[8]}]
61	# FMC LA29_N → K17
62	set_property PACKAGE_PIN K17 [get_ports {ft601_data[9]}]
63	# FMC LA28_P → H17
64	set_property PACKAGE_PIN H17 [get_ports {ft601_data[10]}]
65	# FMC LA29_P → J17
66	set_property PACKAGE_PIN J17 [get_ports {ft601_data[11]}]
67	# FMC LA24_N → L15
68	set_property PACKAGE_PIN L15 [get_ports {ft601_data[12]}]
69	# FMC LA25_N → J15
70	set_property PACKAGE_PIN J15 [get_ports {ft601_data[13]}]
71	# FMC LA24_P → L14
72	set_property PACKAGE_PIN L14 [get_ports {ft601_data[14]}]
73	# FMC LA25_P → H15
74	set_property PACKAGE_PIN H15 [get_ports {ft601_data[15]}]
75	# FMC LA27_N → G13
76	set_property PACKAGE_PIN G13 [get_ports {ft601_data[16]}]
77	# FMC LA26_N → G15
78	set_property PACKAGE_PIN G15 [get_ports {ft601_data[17]}]
79	# FMC LA27_P → H13
80	set_property PACKAGE_PIN H13 [get_ports {ft601_data[18]}]
81	# FMC LA26_P → G16
82	set_property PACKAGE_PIN G16 [get_ports {ft601_data[19]}]
83	# FMC LA21_N → G18
84	set_property PACKAGE_PIN G18 [get_ports {ft601_data[20]}]
85	# FMC LA22_N → L18
86	set_property PACKAGE_PIN L18 [get_ports {ft601_data[21]}]
87	# FMC LA21_P → G17
88	set_property PACKAGE_PIN G17 [get_ports {ft601_data[22]}]
89	# FMC LA22_P → M18
90	set_property PACKAGE_PIN M18 [get_ports {ft601_data[23]}]
91	# FMC LA23_N → H14
92	set_property PACKAGE_PIN H14 [get_ports {ft601_data[24]}]
93	# FMC LA23_P → J14
94	set_property PACKAGE_PIN J14 [get_ports {ft601_data[25]}]
95	# FMC LA19_N → N18
96	set_property PACKAGE_PIN N18 [get_ports {ft601_data[26]}]
97	# FMC LA19_P → N19
98	set_property PACKAGE_PIN N19 [get_ports {ft601_data[27]}]
99	# FMC LA20_N → K14
100	set_property PACKAGE_PIN K14 [get_ports {ft601_data[28]}]
101	# FMC LA20_P → K13
102	set_property PACKAGE_PIN K13 [get_ports {ft601_data[29]}]
103	# FMC LA17_N_CC → H19
104	set_property PACKAGE_PIN H19 [get_ports {ft601_data[30]}]
105	# FMC LA17_P_CC → J19
106	set_property PACKAGE_PIN J19 [get_ports {ft601_data[31]}]
107	
108	set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}]
109	set_property SLEW FAST [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}]
110	set_property DRIVE 8 [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}]
111	
112	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	# FT601 Byte Enable [3:0] — active-low, bidirectional
114	# BE_N[0:1] in Bank 16, BE_N[2:3] in Bank 16
115	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
116	# FMC LA15_N → B20
117	set_property PACKAGE_PIN B20 [get_ports {ft601_be[0]}]
118	# FMC LA15_P → A20
119	set_property PACKAGE_PIN A20 [get_ports {ft601_be[1]}]
120	# FMC LA09_N → D16
121	set_property PACKAGE_PIN D16 [get_ports {ft601_be[2]}]
122	# FMC LA09_P → E16
123	set_property PACKAGE_PIN E16 [get_ports {ft601_be[3]}]
124	
125	set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_be[*]}]
126	set_property SLEW FAST [get_ports {ft601_be[*]}]
127	set_property DRIVE 8 [get_ports {ft601_be[*]}]
128	
129	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
130	# FT601 Control Signals — active-low strobes (Bank 16)
131	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
132	# FMC LA00_P_CC → C17 (OE_N)
133	set_property PACKAGE_PIN C17 [get_ports {ft601_oe_n}]
134	# FMC LA00_N_CC → D17 (RD_N)
135	set_property PACKAGE_PIN D17 [get_ports {ft601_rd_n}]
136	# FMC LA08_P → E13 (WR_N)
137	set_property PACKAGE_PIN E13 [get_ports {ft601_wr_n}]
138	# FMC LA08_N → E14 (SIWU_N)
139	set_property PACKAGE_PIN E14 [get_ports {ft601_siwu_n}]
140	
141	set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_oe_n}]
142	set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_rd_n}]
143	set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_wr_n}]
144	set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_siwu_n}]
145	set_property SLEW FAST [get_ports {ft601_oe_n}]
146	set_property SLEW FAST [get_ports {ft601_rd_n}]
147	set_property SLEW FAST [get_ports {ft601_wr_n}]
148	set_property DRIVE 8 [get_ports {ft601_oe_n}]
149	set_property DRIVE 8 [get_ports {ft601_rd_n}]
150	set_property DRIVE 8 [get_ports {ft601_wr_n}]
151	
152	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
153	# FT601 Status Signals (Bank 16)
154	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
155	# On UMFT601X-B, FT601 drives TXE_N and RXF_N (active-low) to FPGA.
156	# In the production RTL (usb_data_interface.v):
157	#   - ft601_txe  (input, active-HIGH) — FSM checks `!ft601_txe` for "can write"
158	#   - ft601_txe_n (output reg) — driven to 1, unused (production PCB artifact)
159	#   - ft601_rxf  (input, active-HIGH) — not used in write-only FSM
160	#   - ft601_rxf_n (output reg) — driven to 1, unused (production PCB artifact)
161	#
162	# On UMFT601X-B FMC path:
163	#   - TXE_N from FT601 is LOW when FIFO ready → wire to ft601_txe RTL input
164	#   - The FSM checks `!ft601_txe`: !LOW = 1 = proceed. Polarity is CORRECT.
165	#   - ft601_txe_n output and ft601_rxf_n output have no FMC connection (leave
166	#     unconstrained or tie off in RTL wrapper).
167	#
168	# FMC LA07_N → E17 (TXE_N from FT601 — wire to RTL port ft601_txe)
169	set_property PACKAGE_PIN E17 [get_ports {ft601_txe}]
170	# FMC LA07_P → F16 (RXF_N from FT601 — wire to RTL port ft601_rxf)
171	set_property PACKAGE_PIN F16 [get_ports {ft601_rxf}]
172	
173	set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_txe}]
174	set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_rxf}]
175	
176	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
177	# FT601 Reset and Wake (Bank 16) — active-low
178	# These are FPGA-to-FT601 control signals, active-low.
179	# NOTE: The RTL port ft601_reset_n is an internal synchronized reset INPUT,
180	# not a pin output. This FMC pin drives the FT601 chip's RESET_N.
181	# Use a separate port name (ft601_chip_reset_n) to avoid collision.
182	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
183	# FMC LA10_N → A14 (RESET_N — FPGA drives FT601 reset)
184	set_property PACKAGE_PIN A14 [get_ports {ft601_chip_reset_n}]
185	# FMC LA10_P → A13 (WAKEUP_N — FPGA drives FT601 wakeup)
186	set_property PACKAGE_PIN A13 [get_ports {ft601_wakeup_n}]
187	
188	set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_chip_reset_n}]
189	set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_wakeup_n}]
190	
191	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
192	# FT601 GPIO (Bank 16) — directly on FMC, active by default on UMFT601X-B
193	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
194	# FMC LA14_P → A18 (GPIO0)
195	set_property PACKAGE_PIN A18 [get_ports {ft601_gpio0}]
196	# FMC LA14_N → A19 (GPIO1)
197	set_property PACKAGE_PIN A19 [get_ports {ft601_gpio1}]
198	
199	set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_gpio0}]
200	set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_gpio1}]
201	
202	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
203	# FT601 Clock Output (forwarded clock to FT601)
204	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
205	# NOTE: The UMFT601X-B provides its own 100 MHz clock via D_CLK (ft601_clk_in
206	# above). The production design forwards a clock back via ODDR. On the FMC
207	# path, there is NO dedicated return-clock LA pin on the UMFT601X-B.
208	#
209	# The FT601 FIFO interface is source-synchronous from the FT601 perspective:
210	# the FT601 provides D_CLK and the FPGA samples/drives data relative to it.
211	# For WRITE operations the FPGA drives data on D_CLK edges (no separate
212	# forwarded clock needed). ft601_clk_out is NOT used on the FMC path.
213	#
214	# If the RTL requires ft601_clk_out as a port, constrain it to an unused pin
215	# or remove it from the top-level for the FMC build variant.
216	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
217	
218	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
219	# FT601 input delays (relative to ft601_clk_in, 100 MHz)
220	# FT601 datasheet (245 Sync FIFO mode): Tco max = 7.0 ns, Tco min = 1.0 ns
221	# NOTE: ft601_data is bidirectional — input delay applies during READ ops.
222	# ft601_be is output-only in this write-only design; no set_input_delay.
223	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
224	set_input_delay -clock [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -max 7.000 [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}]
225	set_input_delay -clock [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -min 1.000 [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}]
226	set_input_delay -clock [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -max 7.000 [get_ports {ft601_txe}]
227	set_input_delay -clock [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -min 1.000 [get_ports {ft601_txe}]
228	set_input_delay -clock [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -max 7.000 [get_ports {ft601_rxf}]
229	set_input_delay -clock [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -min 1.000 [get_ports {ft601_rxf}]
230	# ft601_be[*] is output-only — removed erroneous set_input_delay (was causing
231	# critical warnings and dropped constraints in previous build)
232	
233	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
234	# FT601 output timing — source-synchronous, datapath-only constraints
235	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
236	# The FT601 provides its own 100 MHz clock (D_CLK) and samples data on
237	# the next rising edge of that same clock. The FPGA receives D_CLK through
238	# IBUF→BUFG (~5 ns insertion), but this insertion delay is COMMON to both
239	# the data launch (register clocked by BUFG output) and the data capture
240	# (FT601 sampling on the same physical clock edge).
241	#
242	# Using set_output_delay with the real clock creates a false ~5 ns skew
243	# penalty. Using a virtual clock doesn't help because Vivado still sees
244	# the IBUF+BUFG insertion on the source side.
245	#
246	# Instead, we use set_max_delay -datapath_only to constrain the
247	# register-to-pad delay directly. The budget is:
248	#   Tperiod(10 ns) - Tsu(2.0 ns) - Tboard_margin(0.5 ns) = 7.5 ns
249	# This ensures data arrives at the FT601 pad with 2.5 ns margin before
250	# the next clock edge.
251	#
252	# For hold (min delay): We use set_min_delay. The FT601 Th = 0.5 ns.
253	# The data must not change until 0.5 ns after the CURRENT clock edge.
254	# Since the register launches on the clock edge and the pad delay is
255	# always positive (>= 1 ns), hold is inherently met. We set min = 0
256	# to be safe.
257	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
258	
259	# Remove any output_delay on these ports (clean slate — the constraints
260	# below supersede them)
261	# Data bus
262	set_max_delay -datapath_only -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}] 7.500
263	set_min_delay -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}] 0.000
264	# Byte enable
265	set_max_delay -datapath_only -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_be[*]}] 7.500
266	set_min_delay -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_be[*]}] 0.000
267	# Write strobe
268	set_max_delay -datapath_only -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_wr_n}] 7.500
269	set_min_delay -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_wr_n}] 0.000
270	# Read strobe
271	set_max_delay -datapath_only -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_rd_n}] 7.500
272	set_min_delay -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_rd_n}] 0.000
273	# Output enable
274	set_max_delay -datapath_only -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_oe_n}] 7.500
275	set_min_delay -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_oe_n}] 0.000
276	
277	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
278	# Note: Input delays still reference ft601_clk_in (correct for inputs,
279	# where the FT601 drives data relative to its clock and the FPGA samples
280	# after IBUF+BUFG insertion — the pessimistic direction Vivado handles
281	# correctly).
282	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
283	
284	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
285	# IOB packing for timing — same strategy as production XDC
286	# Use -quiet because register names depend on synthesis elaboration;
287	# a -quiet miss is non-fatal, but the constraint logs should be checked.
288	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
289	set_property -quiet IOB TRUE [get_cells -hierarchical -filter {NAME =~ *usb_inst/ft601_data_out_reg*}]
290	set_property -quiet IOB TRUE [get_cells -hierarchical -filter {NAME =~ *usb_inst/ft601_be_reg*}]
291	set_property -quiet IOB TRUE [get_cells -hierarchical -filter {NAME =~ *usb_inst/ft601_wr_n_reg*}]
292	set_property -quiet IOB TRUE [get_cells -hierarchical -filter {NAME =~ *usb_inst/ft601_rd_n_reg*}]
293	set_property -quiet IOB TRUE [get_cells -hierarchical -filter {NAME =~ *usb_inst/ft601_oe_n_reg*}]
294	set_property -quiet IOB TRUE [get_cells -hierarchical -filter {NAME =~ *usb_inst/ft601_siwu_n_reg*}]
295	
296	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
297	# Async / false paths — chip reset and wakeup are not timing-critical
298	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
299	set_false_path -to [get_ports {ft601_chip_reset_n}]
300	set_false_path -to [get_ports {ft601_wakeup_n}]
301	set_false_path -to [get_ports {ft601_gpio0}]
302	set_false_path -to [get_ports {ft601_gpio1}]
303	
304	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
305	# NOTES ON RTL ADAPTATION FOR FMC BUILD
306	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
307	# The production RTL (usb_data_interface.v) has these ports that need
308	# handling for the FMC dev build:
309	#
310	# 1. ft601_clk_out (output) — ODDR forwarded clock. No FMC return path
311	#    on UMFT601X-B. Leave unconnected or assign to an unused Bank 16 pin.
312	#
313	# 2. ft601_txe_n (output reg) — Production PCB artifact. Driven to 1,
314	#    never functionally used. Leave unconstrained (no FMC connection).
315	#
316	# 3. ft601_rxf_n (output reg) — Same as txe_n. Leave unconstrained.
317	#
318	# 4. ft601_srb[1:0] (input) — FIFO buffer select. Not on UMFT601X-B FMC.
319	#    Tie to 2'b00 in RTL wrapper or via pulldown.
320	#
321	# 5. ft601_swb[1:0] (input) — Same as srb. Tie to 2'b00.
322	#
323	# 6. ft601_txe (input) — Wired to UMFT601X-B TXE_N (active-low from FT601).
324	#    Polarity works: FSM checks `!ft601_txe` → !LOW=1 → proceed when ready.
325	#
326	# 7. ft601_rxf (input) — Wired to UMFT601X-B RXF_N (active-low from FT601).
327	#    Not used in current write-only FSM but correct for future read path.
328	#
329	# 8. ft601_reset_n (input in RTL at port level, but also used as
330	#    ft601_reset_n on UMFT601X-B FMC LA10_N). The XDC above constrains
331	#    ft601_reset_n — this is an OUTPUT from FPGA to reset the FT601 chip,
332	#    not the RTL's synchronized reset input. The RTL's ft601_reset_n input
333	#    should be driven by internal logic (e.g., system reset synchronized to
334	#    ft601_clk_in). The FMC pin ft601_reset_n should be a separate port.
335	#
336	# RECOMMENDED: Create a thin FMC wrapper module that:
337	#   - Instantiates usb_data_interface with existing port names
338	#   - Ties ft601_srb/ft601_swb to 2'b00
339	#   - Leaves ft601_txe_n/ft601_rxf_n unconnected
340	#   - Assigns ft601_clk_out to an unused pin or removes it
341	#   - Adds ft601_reset_n, ft601_wakeup_n, ft601_gpio0/1 as new top ports
342	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

# ============================================================================
# AERIS-10  FT601 via FMC LPC: TE0713 + TE0701 + UMFT601X-B
# ============================================================================
# Target: XC7A200T-2FBG484C (TE0713-03) on TE0701-06 carrier
# FT601 board: UMFT601X-B (32-bit FT601 eval, FMC LPC)
#
# Signal chain:
#   FPGA ball → TE0713 B2B → TE0701 carrier → FMC LPC J10 → UMFT601X-B FT601
#
# Bank split:
#   Bank 15 (VCCO = VIOTB): DATA[31:0], D_CLK (33 pins)
#   Bank 16 (VCCO = VIOTB): BE_N[3:0], OE_N, RD_N, WR_N, TXE_N, RXF_N,
#                            SIWU_N, RESET_N, WAKEUP_N, GPIO0, GPIO1 (14 pins)
#
# CRITICAL SETUP:
#   1. TE0701 VIOTB must be set to 3.3V (jumper J16/J17/J21 configuration)
#      OR FMC_VADJ (DIP S4) set to 3.3V with VIOTB routed to FMC_VADJ
#   2. UMFT601X-B VCCIO jumper set to 3.3V
#   3. This XDC replaces the FT601 section of xc7a200t_fbg484.xdc (production
#      PCB pinout) — do NOT use both simultaneously
#
# Source mapping:
#   UMFT601X-B: DS_UMFT60x.pdf Table 2.7 (CN4 FMC connector, FT601 column)
#   TE0701→TE0713: TE0701_FMC_PINOUT.xlsx (FMC J10 → B2B → FPGA pin)
#
# Verified: 2026-03-19
# ============================================================================

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FT601 Clock Input — 100 MHz from FT601 chip
# FMC: LA18_P_CC → FPGA J20 (Bank 15, IO_L11P_T1_SRCC_15)
# SRCC is sufficient for 100 MHz FIFO clock via IBUF→BUFG
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
set_property PACKAGE_PIN J20 [get_ports {ft601_clk_in}]
set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_clk_in}]
create_clock -name ft601_clk_in -period 10.000 [get_ports {ft601_clk_in}]
set_input_jitter [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] 0.100

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FT601 Data Bus [31:0] — bidirectional, 3.3V LVCMOS
# All data pins in Bank 15
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FMC LA32_N → L21
set_property PACKAGE_PIN L21 [get_ports {ft601_data[0]}]
# FMC LA33_N → N20
set_property PACKAGE_PIN N20 [get_ports {ft601_data[1]}]
# FMC LA32_P → M21
set_property PACKAGE_PIN M21 [get_ports {ft601_data[2]}]
# FMC LA33_P → M20
set_property PACKAGE_PIN M20 [get_ports {ft601_data[3]}]
# FMC LA30_N → M13
set_property PACKAGE_PIN M13 [get_ports {ft601_data[4]}]
# FMC LA31_N → N22
set_property PACKAGE_PIN N22 [get_ports {ft601_data[5]}]
# FMC LA30_P → L13
set_property PACKAGE_PIN L13 [get_ports {ft601_data[6]}]
# FMC LA31_P → M22
set_property PACKAGE_PIN M22 [get_ports {ft601_data[7]}]
# FMC LA28_N → H18
set_property PACKAGE_PIN H18 [get_ports {ft601_data[8]}]
# FMC LA29_N → K17
set_property PACKAGE_PIN K17 [get_ports {ft601_data[9]}]
# FMC LA28_P → H17
set_property PACKAGE_PIN H17 [get_ports {ft601_data[10]}]
# FMC LA29_P → J17
set_property PACKAGE_PIN J17 [get_ports {ft601_data[11]}]
# FMC LA24_N → L15
set_property PACKAGE_PIN L15 [get_ports {ft601_data[12]}]
# FMC LA25_N → J15
set_property PACKAGE_PIN J15 [get_ports {ft601_data[13]}]
# FMC LA24_P → L14
set_property PACKAGE_PIN L14 [get_ports {ft601_data[14]}]
# FMC LA25_P → H15
set_property PACKAGE_PIN H15 [get_ports {ft601_data[15]}]
# FMC LA27_N → G13
set_property PACKAGE_PIN G13 [get_ports {ft601_data[16]}]
# FMC LA26_N → G15
set_property PACKAGE_PIN G15 [get_ports {ft601_data[17]}]
# FMC LA27_P → H13
set_property PACKAGE_PIN H13 [get_ports {ft601_data[18]}]
# FMC LA26_P → G16
set_property PACKAGE_PIN G16 [get_ports {ft601_data[19]}]
# FMC LA21_N → G18
set_property PACKAGE_PIN G18 [get_ports {ft601_data[20]}]
# FMC LA22_N → L18
set_property PACKAGE_PIN L18 [get_ports {ft601_data[21]}]
# FMC LA21_P → G17
set_property PACKAGE_PIN G17 [get_ports {ft601_data[22]}]
# FMC LA22_P → M18
set_property PACKAGE_PIN M18 [get_ports {ft601_data[23]}]
# FMC LA23_N → H14
set_property PACKAGE_PIN H14 [get_ports {ft601_data[24]}]
# FMC LA23_P → J14
set_property PACKAGE_PIN J14 [get_ports {ft601_data[25]}]
# FMC LA19_N → N18
set_property PACKAGE_PIN N18 [get_ports {ft601_data[26]}]
# FMC LA19_P → N19
set_property PACKAGE_PIN N19 [get_ports {ft601_data[27]}]
# FMC LA20_N → K14
set_property PACKAGE_PIN K14 [get_ports {ft601_data[28]}]
# FMC LA20_P → K13
set_property PACKAGE_PIN K13 [get_ports {ft601_data[29]}]
# FMC LA17_N_CC → H19
set_property PACKAGE_PIN H19 [get_ports {ft601_data[30]}]
# FMC LA17_P_CC → J19
set_property PACKAGE_PIN J19 [get_ports {ft601_data[31]}]

set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}]
set_property SLEW FAST [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}]
set_property DRIVE 8 [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FT601 Byte Enable [3:0] — active-low, bidirectional
# BE_N[0:1] in Bank 16, BE_N[2:3] in Bank 16
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FMC LA15_N → B20
set_property PACKAGE_PIN B20 [get_ports {ft601_be[0]}]
# FMC LA15_P → A20
set_property PACKAGE_PIN A20 [get_ports {ft601_be[1]}]
# FMC LA09_N → D16
set_property PACKAGE_PIN D16 [get_ports {ft601_be[2]}]
# FMC LA09_P → E16
set_property PACKAGE_PIN E16 [get_ports {ft601_be[3]}]

set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_be[*]}]
set_property SLEW FAST [get_ports {ft601_be[*]}]
set_property DRIVE 8 [get_ports {ft601_be[*]}]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FT601 Control Signals — active-low strobes (Bank 16)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FMC LA00_P_CC → C17 (OE_N)
set_property PACKAGE_PIN C17 [get_ports {ft601_oe_n}]
# FMC LA00_N_CC → D17 (RD_N)
set_property PACKAGE_PIN D17 [get_ports {ft601_rd_n}]
# FMC LA08_P → E13 (WR_N)
set_property PACKAGE_PIN E13 [get_ports {ft601_wr_n}]
# FMC LA08_N → E14 (SIWU_N)
set_property PACKAGE_PIN E14 [get_ports {ft601_siwu_n}]

set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_oe_n}]
set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_rd_n}]
set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_wr_n}]
set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_siwu_n}]
set_property SLEW FAST [get_ports {ft601_oe_n}]
set_property SLEW FAST [get_ports {ft601_rd_n}]
set_property SLEW FAST [get_ports {ft601_wr_n}]
set_property DRIVE 8 [get_ports {ft601_oe_n}]
set_property DRIVE 8 [get_ports {ft601_rd_n}]
set_property DRIVE 8 [get_ports {ft601_wr_n}]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FT601 Status Signals (Bank 16)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# On UMFT601X-B, FT601 drives TXE_N and RXF_N (active-low) to FPGA.
# In the production RTL (usb_data_interface.v):
#   - ft601_txe  (input, active-HIGH) — FSM checks `!ft601_txe` for "can write"
#   - ft601_txe_n (output reg) — driven to 1, unused (production PCB artifact)
#   - ft601_rxf  (input, active-HIGH) — not used in write-only FSM
#   - ft601_rxf_n (output reg) — driven to 1, unused (production PCB artifact)
#
# On UMFT601X-B FMC path:
#   - TXE_N from FT601 is LOW when FIFO ready → wire to ft601_txe RTL input
#   - The FSM checks `!ft601_txe`: !LOW = 1 = proceed. Polarity is CORRECT.
#   - ft601_txe_n output and ft601_rxf_n output have no FMC connection (leave
#     unconstrained or tie off in RTL wrapper).
#
# FMC LA07_N → E17 (TXE_N from FT601 — wire to RTL port ft601_txe)
set_property PACKAGE_PIN E17 [get_ports {ft601_txe}]
# FMC LA07_P → F16 (RXF_N from FT601 — wire to RTL port ft601_rxf)
set_property PACKAGE_PIN F16 [get_ports {ft601_rxf}]

set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_txe}]
set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_rxf}]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FT601 Reset and Wake (Bank 16) — active-low
# These are FPGA-to-FT601 control signals, active-low.
# NOTE: The RTL port ft601_reset_n is an internal synchronized reset INPUT,
# not a pin output. This FMC pin drives the FT601 chip's RESET_N.
# Use a separate port name (ft601_chip_reset_n) to avoid collision.
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FMC LA10_N → A14 (RESET_N — FPGA drives FT601 reset)
set_property PACKAGE_PIN A14 [get_ports {ft601_chip_reset_n}]
# FMC LA10_P → A13 (WAKEUP_N — FPGA drives FT601 wakeup)
set_property PACKAGE_PIN A13 [get_ports {ft601_wakeup_n}]

set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_chip_reset_n}]
set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_wakeup_n}]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FT601 GPIO (Bank 16) — directly on FMC, active by default on UMFT601X-B
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FMC LA14_P → A18 (GPIO0)
set_property PACKAGE_PIN A18 [get_ports {ft601_gpio0}]
# FMC LA14_N → A19 (GPIO1)
set_property PACKAGE_PIN A19 [get_ports {ft601_gpio1}]

set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_gpio0}]
set_property IOSTANDARD LVCMOS33 [get_ports {ft601_gpio1}]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FT601 Clock Output (forwarded clock to FT601)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTE: The UMFT601X-B provides its own 100 MHz clock via D_CLK (ft601_clk_in
# above). The production design forwards a clock back via ODDR. On the FMC
# path, there is NO dedicated return-clock LA pin on the UMFT601X-B.
#
# The FT601 FIFO interface is source-synchronous from the FT601 perspective:
# the FT601 provides D_CLK and the FPGA samples/drives data relative to it.
# For WRITE operations the FPGA drives data on D_CLK edges (no separate
# forwarded clock needed). ft601_clk_out is NOT used on the FMC path.
#
# If the RTL requires ft601_clk_out as a port, constrain it to an unused pin
# or remove it from the top-level for the FMC build variant.
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FT601 input delays (relative to ft601_clk_in, 100 MHz)
# FT601 datasheet (245 Sync FIFO mode): Tco max = 7.0 ns, Tco min = 1.0 ns
# NOTE: ft601_data is bidirectional — input delay applies during READ ops.
# ft601_be is output-only in this write-only design; no set_input_delay.
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
set_input_delay -clock [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -max 7.000 [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}]
set_input_delay -clock [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -min 1.000 [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}]
set_input_delay -clock [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -max 7.000 [get_ports {ft601_txe}]
set_input_delay -clock [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -min 1.000 [get_ports {ft601_txe}]
set_input_delay -clock [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -max 7.000 [get_ports {ft601_rxf}]
set_input_delay -clock [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -min 1.000 [get_ports {ft601_rxf}]
# ft601_be[*] is output-only — removed erroneous set_input_delay (was causing
# critical warnings and dropped constraints in previous build)

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# FT601 output timing — source-synchronous, datapath-only constraints
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The FT601 provides its own 100 MHz clock (D_CLK) and samples data on
# the next rising edge of that same clock. The FPGA receives D_CLK through
# IBUF→BUFG (~5 ns insertion), but this insertion delay is COMMON to both
# the data launch (register clocked by BUFG output) and the data capture
# (FT601 sampling on the same physical clock edge).
#
# Using set_output_delay with the real clock creates a false ~5 ns skew
# penalty. Using a virtual clock doesn't help because Vivado still sees
# the IBUF+BUFG insertion on the source side.
#
# Instead, we use set_max_delay -datapath_only to constrain the
# register-to-pad delay directly. The budget is:
#   Tperiod(10 ns) - Tsu(2.0 ns) - Tboard_margin(0.5 ns) = 7.5 ns
# This ensures data arrives at the FT601 pad with 2.5 ns margin before
# the next clock edge.
#
# For hold (min delay): We use set_min_delay. The FT601 Th = 0.5 ns.
# The data must not change until 0.5 ns after the CURRENT clock edge.
# Since the register launches on the clock edge and the pad delay is
# always positive (>= 1 ns), hold is inherently met. We set min = 0
# to be safe.
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Remove any output_delay on these ports (clean slate — the constraints
# below supersede them)
# Data bus
set_max_delay -datapath_only -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}] 7.500
set_min_delay -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_data[*]}] 0.000
# Byte enable
set_max_delay -datapath_only -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_be[*]}] 7.500
set_min_delay -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_be[*]}] 0.000
# Write strobe
set_max_delay -datapath_only -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_wr_n}] 7.500
set_min_delay -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_wr_n}] 0.000
# Read strobe
set_max_delay -datapath_only -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_rd_n}] 7.500
set_min_delay -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_rd_n}] 0.000
# Output enable
set_max_delay -datapath_only -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_oe_n}] 7.500
set_min_delay -from [get_clocks ft601_clk_in] -to [get_ports {ft601_oe_n}] 0.000

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Note: Input delays still reference ft601_clk_in (correct for inputs,
# where the FT601 drives data relative to its clock and the FPGA samples
# after IBUF+BUFG insertion — the pessimistic direction Vivado handles
# correctly).
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# IOB packing for timing — same strategy as production XDC
# Use -quiet because register names depend on synthesis elaboration;
# a -quiet miss is non-fatal, but the constraint logs should be checked.
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
set_property -quiet IOB TRUE [get_cells -hierarchical -filter {NAME =~ *usb_inst/ft601_data_out_reg*}]
set_property -quiet IOB TRUE [get_cells -hierarchical -filter {NAME =~ *usb_inst/ft601_be_reg*}]
set_property -quiet IOB TRUE [get_cells -hierarchical -filter {NAME =~ *usb_inst/ft601_wr_n_reg*}]
set_property -quiet IOB TRUE [get_cells -hierarchical -filter {NAME =~ *usb_inst/ft601_rd_n_reg*}]
set_property -quiet IOB TRUE [get_cells -hierarchical -filter {NAME =~ *usb_inst/ft601_oe_n_reg*}]
set_property -quiet IOB TRUE [get_cells -hierarchical -filter {NAME =~ *usb_inst/ft601_siwu_n_reg*}]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Async / false paths — chip reset and wakeup are not timing-critical
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
set_false_path -to [get_ports {ft601_chip_reset_n}]
set_false_path -to [get_ports {ft601_wakeup_n}]
set_false_path -to [get_ports {ft601_gpio0}]
set_false_path -to [get_ports {ft601_gpio1}]

# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# NOTES ON RTL ADAPTATION FOR FMC BUILD
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The production RTL (usb_data_interface.v) has these ports that need
# handling for the FMC dev build:
#
# 1. ft601_clk_out (output) — ODDR forwarded clock. No FMC return path
#    on UMFT601X-B. Leave unconnected or assign to an unused Bank 16 pin.
#
# 2. ft601_txe_n (output reg) — Production PCB artifact. Driven to 1,
#    never functionally used. Leave unconstrained (no FMC connection).
#
# 3. ft601_rxf_n (output reg) — Same as txe_n. Leave unconstrained.
#
# 4. ft601_srb[1:0] (input) — FIFO buffer select. Not on UMFT601X-B FMC.
#    Tie to 2'b00 in RTL wrapper or via pulldown.
#
# 5. ft601_swb[1:0] (input) — Same as srb. Tie to 2'b00.
#
# 6. ft601_txe (input) — Wired to UMFT601X-B TXE_N (active-low from FT601).
#    Polarity works: FSM checks `!ft601_txe` → !LOW=1 → proceed when ready.
#
# 7. ft601_rxf (input) — Wired to UMFT601X-B RXF_N (active-low from FT601).
#    Not used in current write-only FSM but correct for future read path.
#
# 8. ft601_reset_n (input in RTL at port level, but also used as
#    ft601_reset_n on UMFT601X-B FMC LA10_N). The XDC above constrains
#    ft601_reset_n — this is an OUTPUT from FPGA to reset the FT601 chip,
#    not the RTL's synchronized reset input. The RTL's ft601_reset_n input
#    should be driven by internal logic (e.g., system reset synchronized to
#    ft601_clk_in). The FMC pin ft601_reset_n should be a separate port.
#
# RECOMMENDED: Create a thin FMC wrapper module that:
#   - Instantiates usb_data_interface with existing port names
#   - Ties ft601_srb/ft601_swb to 2'b00
#   - Leaves ft601_txe_n/ft601_rxf_n unconnected
#   - Assigns ft601_clk_out to an unused pin or removes it
#   - Adds ft601_reset_n, ft601_wakeup_n, ft601_gpio0/1 as new top ports
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------

content.content:
1	# build_te0713_umft601x_dev.tcl
2	#
3	# Vivado batch build for Trenz TE0713/TE0701 with UMFT601X-B over FMC LPC.
4	
5	set script_dir [file dirname [file normalize [info script]]]
6	set project_root [file normalize [file join $script_dir "../.."]]
7	
8	set project_name "aeris10_te0713_umft601x_dev"
9	set build_dir [file join $project_root "vivado_te0713_umft601x_dev"]

# build_te0713_umft601x_dev.tcl
#
# Vivado batch build for Trenz TE0713/TE0701 with UMFT601X-B over FMC LPC.

set script_dir [file dirname [file normalize [info script]]]
set project_root [file normalize [file join $script_dir "../.."]]

set project_name "aeris10_te0713_umft601x_dev"
set build_dir [file join $project_root "vivado_te0713_umft601x_dev"]
set reports_dir [file join $build_dir "reports"]

set top_file [file join $project_root "radar_system_top_te0713_umft601x_dev.v"]
set usb_file [file join $project_root "usb_data_interface.v"]
set xdc_file [file join $project_root "constraints" "te0713_te0701_umft601x.xdc"]

file mkdir $build_dir
file mkdir $reports_dir

create_project -force $project_name $build_dir -part xc7a200tfbg484-2
set_property target_language Verilog [current_project]

add_files -norecurse $top_file
add_files -norecurse $usb_file
add_files -fileset constrs_1 -norecurse $xdc_file

set_property top radar_system_top_te0713_umft601x_dev [current_fileset]
update_compile_order -fileset sources_1

# Use Performance_ExplorePostRoutePhysOpt strategy for timing closure
set_property strategy Performance_ExplorePostRoutePhysOpt [get_runs impl_1]

puts "INFO: Launching implementation to bitstream (Performance_ExplorePostRoutePhysOpt)..."
launch_runs impl_1 -to_step write_bitstream -jobs 8
wait_on_run impl_1

set impl_status [get_property STATUS [get_runs impl_1]]
puts "INFO: impl_1 status: $impl_status"

if {![string match "*Complete*" $impl_status]} {
    error "Implementation did not complete successfully. Status: $impl_status"
}

open_run impl_1

report_clocks -file [file join $reports_dir "clocks.rpt"]
report_clock_interaction -file [file join $reports_dir "clock_interaction.rpt"]
report_timing_summary -report_unconstrained -max_paths 100 -file [file join $reports_dir "timing_summary.rpt"]
report_cdc -details -file [file join $reports_dir "cdc.rpt"]
report_exceptions -file [file join $reports_dir "exceptions.rpt"]
report_drc -file [file join $reports_dir "drc.rpt"]
report_utilization -file [file join $reports_dir "utilization.rpt"]

set bit_file [get_property BITSTREAM.FILE [current_design]]

puts "INFO: Build complete."
puts "INFO: Bitstream: $bit_file"
puts "INFO: Reports:   $reports_dir"
